text: Robotic right hemicolectomy with intracorporeal anastomosis using the V-lock suture for sickle adenocarcinoma.

During a robotic right hemicolectomy, the surgeon identifies the ileocolic pedicle and creates a window beneath it by scoring the mesentery with robotic monopolar curved scissors. The duodenum is visualized and preserved, and the ileocolic pedicle is divided using a vessel sealer for high ligation.

The surgeon identifies the ileocolic pedicle and creates a window beneath it by scoring the mesentery with robotic monopolar curved scissors. The duodenum is visualized and preserved, while the ileocolic pedicle is divided using a vessel sealer for high ligation.

The surgeon identifies and divides the right colic artery and vein using the vessel sealer. The remainder of the right colon is mobilized towards the middle colic vessels through medial to lateral dissection. The retro mesenteric plane is developed, and the right branch of the middle colic artery is taken with the vessel sealer.

The surgeon identifies and divides the right colic artery and vein using the vessel sealer. The right colon is mobilized towards the middle colic vessels through medial to lateral dissection, developing the retro mesenteric plane. The right branch of the middle colic artery is also divided with the vessel sealer.

The surgeon dissects the greater omentum from the transverse colon, entering the lesser sac. The cecum, appendix, terminal ileum, and ascending colon are dissected from the right paracolic gutter. After fully mobilizing the right colon, the robotic stapler transects the transverse colon and ileum following the creation of mesenteric windows with the robotic vessel sealer.

The surgeon dissects the greater omentum from the transverse colon, entering the lesser sac. The cecum, appendix, terminal ileum, and ascending colon are dissected off the right paracolic gutter. After fully mobilizing the right colon, the robotic stapler is used to transect the transverse colon and ileum following the creation of mesenteric windows with the robotic vessel sealer.

The fully mobilized specimen is positioned in the right lower quadrant. The surgeon creates an intracorporeal isoperistaltic ileocolonic anastomosis by making small enterotomies on the ileum and transverse colon using monopolar curved scissors. The robotic stapler is used to complete the anastomosis between the ileum and transverse colon. The common defect is closed with a 15 cm 3-0 V-lock suture.

The fully mobilized specimen is positioned in the right lower quadrant. The surgeon creates an intracorporeal isoperistaltic ileocolonic anastomosis by making small enterotomies on the ileum and transverse colon using monopolar curved scissors. The robotic stapler is used to form the anastomosis between the ileum and transverse colon. The common defect is closed with a 15 cm 3-0 V-lock suture.
The surgical specimen is extracted through a three-centimeter suprapubic incision.
The surgeon extracts the specimen through a three-centimeter suprapubic incision.
